title: Reading and Use of English - Part 6
description: You are going to read an article from which six sentences have been removed. Choose from the sentences A–G the one which fits each gap (37–42). There is one extra sentence which you do not need to use.

content.title: Guardians of the Shore: The History of Lighthouses
content.text: For centuries, the sight of a lighthouse beam sweeping across dark water has been one of the most reassuring images known to sailors. These solitary towers — built on headlands, reefs and exposed rocks — have guided ships safely into harbour and warned them away from destruction for thousands of years. (37) [37] Los faros son uno de los inventos más antiguos al servicio de la navegación. [/37] The ancient lighthouse at Alexandria, the Pharos, was counted among the Seven Wonders of the World, and its design influenced lighthouse construction for more than a thousand years. || In medieval Europe, lighthouse maintenance was often undertaken by monks who tended fires on clifftops as acts of charity toward sailors. (38) [38] En la Europa medieval, el mantenimiento de los faros tenía un carácter religioso. [/38] As maritime trade expanded in the seventeenth and eighteenth centuries, however, the task became too important to leave to informal arrangements. Governments and trading organisations began commissioning permanent, engineered structures with reliable light sources. || The greatest engineering challenge was building lighthouses on exposed offshore rocks. The Eddystone lighthouse, completed in 1759 on a dangerous reef fourteen miles south of Plymouth, was the first of several that demonstrated what was possible. (39) [39] La ingeniería de los faros en rocas expuestas fue un logro extraordinario. [/39] Subsequent engineers refined the methods, developing interlocking granite blocks that could withstand the full force of Atlantic storms. || The quality of the light itself underwent a revolution in the early nineteenth century, when Augustin Fresnel developed his famous lens. The Fresnel lens used a series of concentric glass rings to concentrate candlelight into a beam visible twenty miles or more out to sea. (40) [40] La lente de Fresnel transformó la potencia y el alcance de los faros. [/40] By the middle of the century, virtually every major lighthouse in Europe and North America had been fitted with one. || Automation arrived gradually through the twentieth century. Lighthouse keepers — often living in conditions of extreme isolation for months at a time — were gradually made redundant as electric lights, automatic fog signals and remote monitoring systems took over their duties. (41) [41] La automatización puso fin a la vida de los faros tripulados. [/41] The last manned lighthouse in Britain was automated in 1998, ending a tradition of human habitation that had lasted centuries. || Today, GPS and electronic navigation systems have largely replaced lighthouses as primary navigational aids. (42) [42] A pesar de la tecnología moderna, los faros siguen siendo relevantes. [/42] Sailors, maritime authorities and coastal communities continue to value them — not only as backup systems for when electronics fail, but as irreplaceable landmarks and symbols of the sea.

paragraphs.A: Their stories, often involving months of solitary confinement in a rocking tower, have become part of maritime folklore.
paragraphs.B: Many lighthouses are now heritage sites, drawing visitors who climb their spiral staircases to admire panoramic views.
paragraphs.C: This was not a modern invention; structures built to project light across water date back at least two and a half thousand years.
paragraphs.D: The practical result was a light source far more powerful and consistent than anything available before.
paragraphs.E: The religious orders that maintained them believed the act of saving lives at sea was itself a form of prayer.
paragraphs.F: Its construction required workers to be lowered onto the rock by rope, labouring in brief windows between incoming waves.
paragraphs.G: Yet they have not disappeared entirely, and many argue that they never should.

questions.explanation: C introduce la antigüedad de los faros y los vincula al Faro de Alejandría mencionado a continuación.
E explica la motivación religiosa de los monjes medievales que mantenían los fuegos costeros.
F describe las condiciones extremas de la construcción del faro Eddystone, ilustrando el desafío de edificar en rocas expuestas.
D resume el resultado práctico de la lente de Fresnel — luz más potente y constante — antes de mencionar su adopción generalizada.
A describe las condiciones de aislamiento extremo de los guardas de faros, cuya vida pasó a ser parte del folclore marítimo.
G introduce la persistencia de los faros a pesar del GPS, preparando el argumento final sobre su valor como sistemas de respaldo y símbolos.
